title: Plot MaAsLin3 results — gg_maaslin3_plot
text: Creates ggplot2 visualizations MaAsLin3 differential abundance results. Supports summary plots (default), volcano plots, forest plots.

title: Plot MaAsLin3 results — gg_maaslin3_plot
code: gg_maaslin3_plot(   res,   type = c("summary", "volcano", "forest"),   model = c("abundance", "prevalence", "both"),   metadata_filter = NULL,   signif_threshold = 0.1,   use_qval = TRUE,   top_n = 25,   show_labels = FALSE,   point_size = 2,   colors = NULL,   show_annotations = TRUE,   reference_label = NULL,   coef_plot_vars = NULL,   heatmap_vars = NULL,   normalization = "TSS",   transform = "LOG" )

title: Plot MaAsLin3 results — gg_maaslin3_plot
text: res (list, required) full result object maaslin3_pq(). types "summary", can also directly $results data frame. type (character, default "summary") Type plot. One : "summary": Default MaAsLin3 summary plot coefficients heatmap (uses maaslin3::maaslin_plot_results() internally) "volcano": Effect size (coef) vs -log10(p-value) "forest": Effect sizes confidence intervals model (character, default "abundance") model results plot. One "abundance", "prevalence", "". Used volcano forest plots. metadata_filter (character, default NULL) Filter results specific metadata variable. NULL, uses first non-intercept variable. Used volcano forest plots. signif_threshold (numeric, default 0.1) Significance threshold q-value (FDR-corrected). summary plot, passed max_significance. plots, used color significant features. use_qval (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, use q-values (FDR-corrected) significance. FALSE, use raw p-values. Used volcano forest. top_n (integer, default 25) summary plot, number top features display (summary_plot_first_n). forest plot, show top N features absolute effect size. show_labels (logical, default FALSE) volcano plots, show feature labels significant points. point_size (numeric, default 2) Size points volcano plot. colors (character vector, default NULL) Colors significant/ non-significant points. NULL, uses c("grey60", "firebrick3"). show_annotations (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, adds annotations indicating group higher values side plot. simple formulas like ~ Height, shows "High" vs "Low". reference_label (character, default NULL) Label reference group. NULL, attempts extract metadata column name. coef_plot_vars (character vector, default NULL) summary plot . Variables include coefficient plot. NULL, uses non-intercept variables. heatmap_vars (character vector, default NULL) summary plot . Variables include heatmap. NULL, determined automatically. normalization (character, default "TSS") Normalization method used. needed summary plot. One "TSS", "CLR", "NONE". transform (character, default "LOG") Transform method used. needed summary plot. One "LOG", "PLOG", "NONE".

title: Plot MaAsLin3 results — gg_maaslin3_plot
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Plot MaAsLin3 results — gg_maaslin3_plot
text: #TODO experimental Summary plot (default): Uses maaslin3::maaslin_plot_results() create standard MaAsLin3 visualization sorted per-feature coefficients plotted standard errors, heatmap additional variables. requires full maaslin3 result object (just results data frame). Volcano plot: Shows relationship effect size (coefficient) statistical significance. Points horizontal dashed line significant specified threshold. Points colored significance. Forest plot: Shows effect sizes 95 percent confidence intervals top features. Features ordered effect size. Significant associations highlighted. coef MaAsLin3 abundance models represents log2 fold change: one-unit change variable corresponds 2^coef fold change relative abundance.

title: Plot MaAsLin3 results — gg_maaslin3_plot
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Plot MaAsLin3 results — gg_maaslin3_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Run MaAsLin3 res <- maaslin3_pq(   data_fungi,   formula = "~ Height",   reference = list(Height = "Low") )  # Summary plot (default) - uses maaslin3's native visualization gg_maaslin3_plot(res)  # Volcano plot gg_maaslin3_plot(res, type = "volcano")  # Forest plot of top 15 features gg_maaslin3_plot(res, type = "forest", top_n = 15)  # Plot prevalence model results gg_maaslin3_plot(res, type = "volcano", model = "prevalence")  # Customize significance threshold gg_maaslin3_plot(res, type = "volcano", signif_threshold = 0.05, show_labels = TRUE)   # Complete Example with HMP2 Dataset   library(maaslin3)  # ============================================================  # Convert maaslin3 default HMP2 dataset to phyloseq  # ============================================================   # Read the HMP2 default dataset from maaslin3 package  taxa_table_name <- system.file("extdata", "HMP2_taxonomy.tsv", package = "maaslin3")  taxa_table <- read.csv(taxa_table_name, sep = "\t", row.names = 1)   metadata_name <- system.file("extdata", "HMP2_metadata.tsv", package = "maaslin3")  metadata <- read.csv(metadata_name, sep = "\t", row.names = 1)   # Set factor levels  metadata$antibiotics <- factor(metadata$antibiotics, levels = c("No", "Yes"))   # Create phyloseq object (HMP2 data has samples as rows, taxa as columns)  otu <- otu_table(as.matrix(taxa_table), taxa_are_rows = FALSE)  sam <- sample_data(metadata)  species_names <- colnames(taxa_table)  tax_df <- data.frame(    Species = species_names,    Genus = sapply(strsplit(species_names, "_"), \(x) x[1]),    row.names = species_names  )  tax <- tax_table(as.matrix(tax_df))  physeq_hmp2 <- phyloseq(otu, sam, tax)   # ============================================================  # Run MaAsLin3 analysis  # ============================================================   res <- maaslin3_pq(    physeq_hmp2,    formula = "~ antibiotics",    reference = list(antibiotics = "No"),    output = "output/maaslin3_hmp2",    correction_for_sample_size = FALSE  )   # ============================================================  # Compare plots  # ============================================================   # Summary plot (NEW DEFAULT) - uses maaslin3's native visualization  gg_maaslin3_plot(res)   # Alternative plot types  gg_maaslin3_plot(res, type = "volcano")  gg_maaslin3_plot(res, type = "forest", top_n = 15) } # }

what: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq
title: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq
text: Performs automated model selection multimodel inference using MiscMetabar::glmutli_pq() phyloseq object list_phyloseq. Returns summary table results phyloseq objects.

title: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq
code: glmulti_lpq(   x,   formula,   fitfunction = "lm",   hill_scales = c(0, 1, 2),   aic_step = 2,   confsetsize = 100,   plotty = FALSE,   level = 1,   method = "h",   crit = "aicc",   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq
text: x (required) list_phyloseq object. formula (character, required) model formula glmulti. Variables must present sample_data slot phyloseq objects. Hill numbers (Hill_0, Hill_1, Hill_2) Abundance automatically available. fitfunction (character, default "lm") model fitting function use. Options include "lm" linear models "glm" generalized linear models. hill_scales (numeric vector, default c(0, 1, 2)) q values Hill number computation. Defaults Hill numbers 0 (richness), 1 (Shannon exponential), 2 (inverse Simpson). aic_step (numeric, default 2) AIC score threshold model selection. Models within threshold best model included. confsetsize (integer, default 100) number models return confidence set. plotty (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, display IC profile glmulti search. level (integer, default 1) Model complexity level. 1 main effects , 2 pairwise interactions. method (character, default "h") search method glmulti. Options: "h" (exhaustive), "g" (genetic algorithm), "l" (branch--bound), "d" (summary ). crit (character, default "aicc") Information criterion model selection. Options include "aic", "aicc" (small-sample corrected AIC), "bic". verbose (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, print progress messages. ... Additional arguments passed MiscMetabar::glmutli_pq().

title: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq
text: tibble combined results phyloseq objects, containing following columns: name Name phyloseq object variable variable name model estimates model coefficient estimate unconditional_interval Confidence interval model averaging nb_model Number models containing variable importance Relative importance variable (sum Akaike weights) alpha Significance level

title: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq
text: function requires list_phyloseq type SEPARATE_ANALYSIS, formula must contain variables common across phyloseq objects. function wraps MiscMetabar::glmutli_pq(), wraps glmulti package automated model selection. phyloseq object, Hill diversity indices computed used response variables model selection process.

title: Automated model selection for Hill diversity on each phyloseq in a list_phyloseq — glmulti_lpq

what: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
text: class store compare multiple phyloseq objects. contains: list phyloseq objects summary table computed characteristics phyloseq list comparison characteristics phyloseq objects S7 class store compare multiple phyloseq objects.

title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
code: list_phyloseq(   physeq_list,   names = NULL,   same_primer_seq_tech = TRUE,   same_bioinfo_pipeline = TRUE )

title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
text: physeq_list (required) named list phyloseq objects. names (character vector, default NULL) Optional names phyloseq objects. NULL list unnamed, names generated automatically. same_primer_seq_tech (logical, default TRUE) Whether primer sequencing technology used across phyloseq objects. Set FALSE comparing different primers (e.g., ITS1 vs ITS2) technologies (e.g., Illumina vs PacBio). same_bioinfo_pipeline (logical, default TRUE) Whether bioinformatics pipeline used across phyloseq objects. Set FALSE comparing different clustering methods, taxonomic databases, analysis parameters.

title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
text: combination same_primer_seq_tech same_bioinfo_pipeline along detected sample overlap determines comparison type:

title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
text: phyloseq_list named list phyloseq objects summary_table tibble summarizing phyloseq object comparison list characteristics comparing phyloseq objects

title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
text: list_phyloseq class determines type comparison based : Detected characteristics (sample overlap, nested samples, shared modalities) User-provided parameters (same_primer_seq_tech same_bioinfo_pipeline) six main types comparisons: REPRODUCIBILITY pipeline (same_bioinfo_pipeline = TRUE), primer/technology (same_primer_seq_tech = TRUE), samples. Used test reproducibility results running exact analysis multiple times. ROBUSTNESS Different pipeline (same_bioinfo_pipeline = FALSE, e.g., different clustering method, different assignment database) primer/technology (same_primer_seq_tech = TRUE), samples. Used test robustness conclusions methodological choices. NESTED_ROBUSTNESS One phyloseq object derived another, samples subset (e.g., rarefied version created rarefy_even_depth()). Used test robustness data processing choices like rarefaction, filtering, subsetting. Comparisons focus common (nested) samples. REPLICABILITY Different primer /technology (same_primer_seq_tech = FALSE, e.g., ITS1 vs ITS2, Illumina vs PacBio), samples. Used test replicability across taxonomic groups sequencing technologies. EXPLORATION Different samples shared modalities. Useful explore differences among groups samples. Note: consider merging samples one phyloseq object analyses instead. SEPARATE_ANALYSIS Different samples shared modalities. Separate analysis phyloseq object recommended direct comparison may meaningful.

title: S7 class for comparing phyloseq objects — list_phyloseq
code: # REPRODUCIBILITY: Same samples, same pipeline (default) lpq_repro <- list_phyloseq(list(run1 = data_fungi, run2 = data_fungi)) #> ℹ Building summary table for 2 phyloseq objects... #> ℹ Computing comparison characteristics... #> ℹ Checking sample and taxa overlap... #> ℹ Detected comparison type: REPRODUCIBILITY #> ℹ 185 common samples, 1420 common taxa #> ✔ list_phyloseq created (REPRODUCIBILITY)  # ROBUSTNESS: Same samples, different pipeline lpq_robust <- list_phyloseq(   list(method_A = data_fungi, method_B = data_fungi),   same_bioinfo_pipeline = FALSE ) #> ℹ Building summary table for 2 phyloseq objects... #> ℹ Computing comparison characteristics... #> ℹ Checking sample and taxa overlap... #> ℹ Detected comparison type: ROBUSTNESS #> ℹ 185 common samples, 1420 common taxa #> ✔ list_phyloseq created (ROBUSTNESS)  # REPLICABILITY: Same samples, different primer/technology lpq_replic <- list_phyloseq(   list(ITS1 = data_fungi, ITS2 = data_fungi),   same_primer_seq_tech = FALSE ) #> ℹ Building summary table for 2 phyloseq objects... #> ℹ Computing comparison characteristics... #> ℹ Checking sample and taxa overlap... #> ℹ Detected comparison type: REPLICABILITY #> ℹ 185 common samples, 1420 common taxa #> ✔ list_phyloseq created (REPLICABILITY)

title: Run MaAsLin3 differential abundance analysis on a phyloseq object — maaslin3_pq
code: maaslin3_pq(   physeq,   formula,   reference = NULL,   correction_for_sample_size = TRUE,   output = "res_maaslin3",   ... )

title: Run MaAsLin3 differential abundance analysis on a phyloseq object — maaslin3_pq
text: physeq (phyloseq, required) phyloseq object. formula (character, required) formula string model (e.g., "~ Height" "~ Height + Time"). reference (named list, default NULL) Reference levels categorical variables 2 levels. Format: list(varname = "ref_level"). example: list(Height = "Low") sets "Low" reference Height. Variables list use first level reference. correction_for_sample_size (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, adds nb_seq (library size) formula control sequencing depth. output (character, default "res_maaslin3") Output directory MaAsLin3 results. ... Additional arguments passed maaslin3::maaslin3().

title: Run MaAsLin3 differential abundance analysis on a phyloseq object — maaslin3_pq
text: result object maaslin3::maaslin3(), containing: results: Data frame differential abundance results results_ordered: Results ordered significance MaAsLin3 output components

what: Merge a list_phyloseq into a single phyloseq object — merge_lpq
title: Merge a list_phyloseq into a single phyloseq object — merge_lpq
text: Merges phyloseq objects list_phyloseq single phyloseq object original phyloseq becomes one sample. Abundances summed across samples within phyloseq object. Taxa matched across phyloseq objects either reference sequences (refseq slot, default) taxa names. Matching refseq preferred taxa names often inconsistent across independently built phyloseq objects (e.g., ASV_1 one object taxon ASV_1 another).

title: Merge a list_phyloseq into a single phyloseq object — merge_lpq
code: merge_lpq(   x,   match_by = c("refseq", "names"),   tax_priority = 1L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Merge a list_phyloseq into a single phyloseq object — merge_lpq
text: x (list_phyloseq, required) list_phyloseq object. match_by (character, default "refseq") match taxa across phyloseq objects. One : "refseq": match DNA sequence refseq slot (recommended). phyloseq objects must refseq slot. "names": match taxa names. Use taxa names consistent across objects (e.g., pipeline, database). tax_priority (character integer, default 1L) phyloseq object's taxonomy use taxa matched. Either name list integer index. taxon appears multiple objects, taxonomy priority object used; absent , first available taxonomy used. verbose (logical, default TRUE) Print information merge.

title: Merge a list_phyloseq into a single phyloseq object — merge_lpq
text: phyloseq object : otu_table One column per original phyloseq object (summed across samples), one row per unique taxon. sample_data One row per original phyloseq, column source_name containing list_phyloseq names. tax_table Taxonomy priority object (first available). refseq Present match_by = "refseq".

title: Merge a list_phyloseq into a single phyloseq object — merge_lpq
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Merge a list_phyloseq into a single phyloseq object — merge_lpq
code: lpq <- list_phyloseq(list(   fungi = data_fungi_mini,   fungi2 = data_fungi_mini )) #> ℹ Building summary table for 2 phyloseq objects... #> ℹ Computing comparison characteristics... #> ℹ Checking sample and taxa overlap... #> ℹ Detected comparison type: REPRODUCIBILITY #> ℹ 137 common samples, 45 common taxa #> ✔ list_phyloseq created (REPRODUCIBILITY)  # Merge by refseq (default) merged <- merge_lpq(lpq) #> Merging 2 phyloseq objects by refseq: 45 + 45 taxa -> 45 unique sequences. merged #> phyloseq-class experiment-level object #> otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 45 taxa and 2 samples ] #> sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 2 samples by 2 sample variables ] #> tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 45 taxa by 12 taxonomic ranks ] #> refseq()      DNAStringSet:      [ 45 reference sequences ]  # Merge by taxa names merged_names <- merge_lpq(lpq, match_by = "names") #> Merging 2 phyloseq objects by names: 45 + 45 taxa -> 45 unique names.

what: Simulate microbiome data with differential abundance using MIDASim — midasim_pq
title: Simulate microbiome data with differential abundance using MIDASim — midasim_pq
text: Uses MIDASim package generate realistic simulated microbiome count data known differential abundance effects. function learns structure (taxon correlations, sparsity patterns, library size distribution) template phyloseq object generates new data specified DA taxa.

title: Simulate microbiome data with differential abundance using MIDASim — midasim_pq
code: midasim_pq(   physeq,   fact,   condition,   n_samples = NULL,   prop_case = 0.5,   n_da_taxa = 10,   effect_size = 1,   da_taxa_idx = NULL,   min_prevalence = 0.1,   mode = c("nonparametric", "parametric"),   seed = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Simulate microbiome data with differential abundance using MIDASim — midasim_pq
text: physeq (phyloseq, required) phyloseq object use template. MIDASim learn taxon-taxon correlations abundance distributions data. fact (character, required) Column name sample_data defining binary factor DA simulation. condition (character, required) level fact taxa differentially abundant (case group). n_samples (integer, default NULL) Number samples simulate. NULL, uses number template. prop_case (numeric, default 0.5) Proportion simulated samples belong case group (DA taxa elevated). n_da_taxa (integer, default 10) Number taxa make differentially abundant. effect_size (numeric, default 1) Log-fold change DA taxa case samples. Positive values increase abundance; negative decrease. Typical values: 0.5-2 moderate effects. da_taxa_idx (integer vector, default NULL) Specific taxon indices make DA. NULL, randomly selects n_da_taxa prevalent taxa. min_prevalence (numeric, default 0.1) Minimum prevalence taxon eligible DA selection. mode (character, default "nonparametric") MIDASim mode. One "nonparametric" (realistic) "parametric" (controllable). seed (integer, default NULL) Random seed reproducibility. verbose (logical, default FALSE) Print progress messages.

title: Simulate microbiome data with differential abundance using MIDASim — midasim_pq
text: phyloseq object simulated counts. object includes: Simulated OTU table DA effects Tax table template (taxa preserved) New sample_data binary factor column Attributes: da_taxa_idx (indices), da_taxa_names (names), effect_size, condition

title: Simulate microbiome data with differential abundance using MIDASim — midasim_pq
text: #TODO :  WORKING aldex2 simulation workflow follows MIDASim's three-step process: Setup: MIDASim.setup() learns template's taxon abundance distributions, sparsity patterns, taxon-taxon correlations. Modify: MIDASim.modify() introduces DA effects creating sample-specific relative abundances. case samples (fact == condition), selected taxa multiplied exp(effect_size), renormalized sum 1. Simulate: MIDASim() generates realistic count data preserving template's correlation structure. approach superior simple count multiplication : Taxon-taxon correlations preserved Sparsity patterns realistic Library sizes follow realistic distributions DA signal embedded data generation process

title: Simulate microbiome data with differential abundance using MIDASim — midasim_pq
text: M, et al. (2024). MIDASim: fast simple simulator realistic microbiome data. Microbiome. doi:10.1186/s40168-024-01822-z

title: Simulate microbiome data with differential abundance using MIDASim — midasim_pq
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Simulate microbiome data with differential abundance using MIDASim — midasim_pq
code: # Requires MIDASim package # install.packages("MIDASim")  # Simulate data with 10 DA taxa having log-fold change of 2 sim_pq <- midasim_pq(   data_fungi_mini,   fact = "Height",   condition = "High",   n_da_taxa = 10,   effect_size = 2,   seed = 42 ) #> Taxa are now in columns. #> Template: 137 samples, 45 taxa #> Simulating: 137 samples #> Running MIDASim.setup (mode = nonparametric)... #> The number of samples is larger than the number of taxa, please make sure rows are samples #> Warning: Only 9 taxa meet prevalence threshold. Using all eligible taxa. #> Selected 9 DA taxa #> Running MIDASim.modify... #> Running MIDASim... #> Done. Simulated 137 samples with 9 DA taxa (effect = 2)  # Test with ALDEx2 sim_pq@sam_data$Height <-   as.character(sim_pq@sam_data$Height) res <- MiscMetabar::aldex_pq(   sim_pq,   bifactor = "Height",   modalities = c("Low", "High") ) #> Taxa are now in rows. #> aldex.clr: generating Monte-Carlo instances and clr values #> conditions vector supplied #> operating in serial mode #> aldex.scaleSim: adjusting samples to reflect scale uncertainty. #> aldex.ttest: doing t-test #> aldex.effect: calculating effect sizes gg_aldex_plot(res, type = "volcano")   # Check which taxa were set as DA attr(sim_pq, "da_taxa_names") #> [1] "ASV7"  "ASV8"  "ASV12" "ASV18" "ASV25" "ASV34" "ASV71" "ASV83" "ASV94"  res_height <- ancombc_pq(   sim_pq,   fact = "Height",   levels_fact = c("Low", "High"),   verbose = TRUE, tax_level = NULL ) #> Checking the input data type ... #> The input data is of type: TreeSummarizedExperiment #> PASS #> Checking the sample metadata ... #> The specified variables in the formula: Height #> The available variables in the sample metadata: Height #> PASS #> Checking other arguments ... #> The number of groups of interest is: 2 #> Warning: The group variable has < 3 categories  #> The multi-group comparisons (global/pairwise/dunnet/trend) will be deactivated #> The sample size per group is: Low = 69, High = 68 #> PASS #> Warning: The number of taxa used for estimating sample-specific biases is: 10 #> A large number of taxa (>50) is required for the consistent estimation of biases #> Obtaining initial estimates ... #> Estimating sample-specific biases ... #> Warning: Estimation of sampling fractions failed for the following samples: #> sim_sample_5, sim_sample_21, sim_sample_24, sim_sample_26, sim_sample_34, sim_sample_38, sim_sample_44, sim_sample_48, sim_sample_54, sim_sample_68, sim_sample_72, sim_sample_81, sim_sample_100, sim_sample_101, sim_sample_106, sim_sample_112, sim_sample_117, sim_sample_130 #> These samples may have an excessive number of zero values #> ANCOM-BC2 primary results ... #> Conducting sensitivity analysis for pseudo-count addition to 0s ... #> For taxa that are significant but do not pass the sensitivity analysis, #> they are marked in the 'passed_ss' column and will be treated as non-significant in the 'diff_robust' column. #> For detailed instructions on performing sensitivity analysis, please refer to the package vignette.  ggplot(   res_height$res,   aes(     y = reorder(taxon, lfc_HeightHigh),     x = lfc_HeightHigh,     color = diff_HeightHigh   ) ) +   geom_vline(xintercept = 0) +   geom_segment(aes(     xend = 0, y = reorder(taxon, lfc_HeightHigh),     yend = reorder(taxon, lfc_HeightHigh)   ), color = "darkgrey") +   geom_point()

what: Indicator species analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — multipatt_lpq
title: Indicator species analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — multipatt_lpq
text: Performs indicator species analysis using indicspecies::multipatt() phyloseq object list_phyloseq returns combined result table significant indicator taxa.

title: Indicator species analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — multipatt_lpq
code: multipatt_lpq(   x,   fact,   p_adjust_method = "BH",   pval = 0.05,   control = permute::how(nperm = 999),   verbose = TRUE,   ... )

title: Indicator species analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — multipatt_lpq
text: x (required) list_phyloseq object. fact (character, required) name column sample_data use grouping factor. Must present phyloseq objects. p_adjust_method (character, default "BH") p-value adjustment method. See stats::p.adjust() available methods. pval (numeric, default 0.05) significance threshold adjusted p-values. control (list, default permute::(nperm = 999)) Permutation control settings permutation test. verbose (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, print progress messages. ... Additional arguments passed indicspecies::multipatt().

title: Indicator species analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — multipatt_lpq
text: tibble combined significant indicator taxa phyloseq objects. Contains columns multipatt()$sign output plus taxon (taxon name), p.adj (adjusted p-value), name (identifying source phyloseq object). taxa p.adj < pval included.

title: Indicator species analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — multipatt_lpq
text: function requires list_phyloseq type SEPARATE_ANALYSIS, factor must common across phyloseq objects. Unlike MiscMetabar::multipatt_pq() returns plot, function returns underlying data tibble, making easier compare results across phyloseq objects. common taxa exist across phyloseq objects, taxa names suffixed phyloseq object name make distinguishable.

title: Indicator species analysis on each phyloseq object in a list_phyloseq — multipatt_lpq
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ lpq <- list_phyloseq(   list(     fungi = data_fungi,     fungi_clust = postcluster_pq(data_fungi)   ),   same_bioinfo_pipeline = FALSE )  results <- multipatt_lpq(lpq, fact = "Height") results } # }

what: Multiply OTU counts conditionally based on sample metadata — multiply_counts_pq
title: Multiply OTU counts conditionally based on sample metadata — multiply_counts_pq
text: Multiplies OTU table counts different values depending level factor column sample_data. Samples whose factor value match condition (including NA samples) left unchanged unless explicitly targeted.

title: Multiply OTU counts conditionally based on sample metadata — multiply_counts_pq
code: multiply_counts_pq(   physeq,   fact,   conditions,   multipliers,   prop_taxa = 0.5,   seed = NULL,   compensate = FALSE,   min_prevalence = 0,   round = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Multiply OTU counts conditionally based on sample metadata — multiply_counts_pq
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. fact (character, required) name column sample_data(physeq). conditions (character vector, required) Values fact match. Use NA target samples missing values. multipliers (numeric vector, required) Multiplying factors corresponding element conditions. Must length conditions. prop_taxa (numeric, default 0.5) Proportion taxa (0 1) multiplication applied. Taxa randomly sampled. Set 1 apply taxa. seed (integer, default NULL) Random seed reproducible taxa sampling prop_taxa < 1. compensate (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, scale non-selected taxa total library size per sample preserved. creates pure compositional shift: selected taxa gain relative abundance rest lose . mode essential testing differential abundance methods (ALDEx2, ANCOM-BC) normalize library size. min_prevalence (numeric, default 0) Minimum prevalence (proportion matched samples non-zero counts) taxon eligible selection. Setting > 0 (e.g. 0.5) ensures taxa actually present target samples inflated, producing stronger realistic DA signal. round (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, round resulting counts integers (since OTU tables typically contain integer counts). verbose (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, print message number modified taxa.

title: Multiply OTU counts conditionally based on sample metadata — multiply_counts_pq
text: phyloseq object modified OTU counts. selected taxa stored attribute accessible via attr(result, "taxa_modified").

title: Multiply OTU counts conditionally based on sample metadata — multiply_counts_pq
text: sample checked conditions order. sample's value fact matches condition, counts multiplied corresponding multiplier. Samples match condition left unchanged. prop_taxa < 1, random subset taxa affected multiplication. useful simulate differential abundance taxa respond condition. Simulating differential abundance DA methods: DA methods like ALDEx2 ANCOM-BC work compositions (relative abundances). Simply multiplying counts changes library size, methods normalize away. Use compensate = TRUE create real compositional shift: selected taxa inflated, non- selected taxa scaled total counts per sample stay constant. Combine min_prevalence > 0 small prop_taxa (e.g. 0.05) realistic DA signal , prevalent taxa. Compensation limits: multiplier high selected taxa already high counts, non-selected taxa may enough counts compensate. cases, non-selected taxa zeroed selected taxa scaled preserve library size. warning issued occurs. avoid , use moderate multipliers (2-3) select taxa lower initial abundance. target NA values factor column, include NA conditions (e.g., conditions = c("High", NA)).

title: Multiply OTU counts conditionally based on sample metadata — multiply_counts_pq
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Multiply OTU counts conditionally based on sample metadata — multiply_counts_pq
code: # Multiply counts by 1.2 for "High" samples in half of taxa d <- multiply_counts_pq(data_fungi,   fact = "Height",   conditions = "High", multipliers = 2 ) #> Modified 710 taxa in 41 matched samples  # Multiple conditions with different multipliers d2 <- multiply_counts_pq(data_fungi,   fact = "Height",   conditions = c("High", "Low"), multipliers = c(1.5, 0.8) ) #> Modified 710 taxa in 86 matched samples  # Target NA samples d3 <- multiply_counts_pq(data_fungi,   fact = "Height",   conditions = c("High", NA), multipliers = c(1.2, 0.5) ) #> Modified 710 taxa in 95 matched samples  sum(data_fungi@otu_table) #> [1] 1839124 sum(d@otu_table) #> [1] 2011678 sum(d2@otu_table) #> [1] 1904779 sum(d3@otu_table) #> [1] 1669433  # Apply only to 30% of taxa with a reproducible seed set d4 <- multiply_counts_pq(data_fungi,   fact = "Height",   conditions = "High", multipliers = 2, prop_taxa = 0.3, seed = 42 ) #> Modified 426 taxa in 41 matched samples  # Simulate DA for compositional methods (ALDEx2, ANCOM-BC): # Use compensate=TRUE to create a real compositional shift # Use min_prevalence to select only prevalent taxa # Use small prop_taxa to affect few taxa strongly d_da <- multiply_counts_pq(   data_fungi,   fact = "Height",   conditions = "High",   multipliers = 5,   prop_taxa = 0.05,   min_prevalence = 0.5,   compensate = TRUE,   seed = 123 ) #> Warning: 4 sample(s) could not be fully compensated (multiplier too high relative to non-selected taxa abundance). Non-selected taxa were zeroed and selected taxa were scaled down to preserve library size. #> Modified 1 taxa in 41 matched samples  # Library sizes are preserved sum(sample_sums(data_fungi)) #> [1] what: Count unique taxonomic levels across phyloseq objects — n_levels_lpq
title: Count unique taxonomic levels across phyloseq objects — n_levels_lpq
text: Creates summary table showing number unique taxonomic values (levels) taxonomic rank across phyloseq objects list_phyloseq. useful comparing taxonomic resolution diversity across different datasets classification methods.

title: Count unique taxonomic levels across phyloseq objects — n_levels_lpq
code: n_levels_lpq(x, taxonomic_ranks, na.rm = TRUE)

title: Count unique taxonomic levels across phyloseq objects — n_levels_lpq
text: x (required) list_phyloseq object. taxonomic_ranks (character vector, required) Names taxonomic ranks count. Must present tax_table phyloseq objects list. na.rm (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, NA values excluded counting unique levels.

title: Count unique taxonomic levels across phyloseq objects — n_levels_lpq
text: data frame : Rows One row per phyloseq object (named phyloseq name) Columns One column per taxonomic rank, containing count unique values rank phyloseq object

title: Count unique taxonomic levels across phyloseq objects — n_levels_lpq
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Count unique taxonomic levels across phyloseq objects — n_levels_lpq
code: lpq <- list_phyloseq(list(fungi = data_fungi, fungi_mini = data_fungi_mini)) #> ℹ Building summary table for 2 phyloseq objects... #> ℹ Computing comparison characteristics... #> ℹ Checking sample and taxa overlap... #> ℹ Detected comparison type: NESTED_ROBUSTNESS #> ℹ 137 common samples, 45 common taxa #> ✔ list_phyloseq created (NESTED_ROBUSTNESS)  n_levels_lpq(lpq, c("Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus")) #>            Phylum Class Order Family Genus #> fungi           6    25    73    163   252 #> fungi_mini      1     3     9     20    22

what: Simulate differential abundance by redistributing OTU counts — permute_da_pq
title: Simulate differential abundance by redistributing OTU counts — permute_da_pq
text: Creates fake differential abundance signal redistributing counts within matched samples: selected "DA" taxa receive counts taken non-selected taxa. Library sizes (row sums) preserved, taxa totals (column sums) allowed change create detectable DA signal. approach suitable testing DA methods (ALDEx2, ANCOM-BC) creates true compositional shift maintaining library size normalization methods rely .

title: Simulate differential abundance by redistributing OTU counts — permute_da_pq
code: permute_da_pq(   physeq,   fact,   conditions,   effect_size = 3,   prop_taxa = 0.05,   min_prevalence = 0.1,   seed = NULL,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Simulate differential abundance by redistributing OTU counts — permute_da_pq
text: physeq (phyloseq, required) phyloseq object. fact (character, required) Column name sample_data grouping. conditions (character vector, required) Levels fact DA simulated. effect_size (numeric, default 3) fold-change relative abundance selected taxa matched samples. Values > 1 increase abundance. prop_taxa (numeric, default 0.05) Proportion taxa selected DA taxa. min_prevalence (numeric, default 0.1) Minimum prevalence matched samples taxon eligible. seed (integer, default NULL) Random seed reproducibility. verbose (logical, default FALSE) Print progress info.

title: Simulate differential abundance by redistributing OTU counts — permute_da_pq
text: phyloseq object modified OTU counts creating DA signal. selected DA taxa stored attr(result, "da_taxa").

title: Simulate differential abundance by redistributing OTU counts — permute_da_pq
text: algorithm works follows: Select subset prevalent taxa "DA" taxa matched samples : Multiply selected taxa counts effect_size Scale non-selected taxa preserve library size Round integers Unlike multiply_counts_pq(), function guarantees library sizes exactly preserved (except rounding), creating pure compositional shift DA methods can detect.

title: Simulate differential abundance by redistributing OTU counts — permute_da_pq
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Simulate differential abundance by redistributing OTU counts — permute_da_pq
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ d_da <- permute_da_pq(   data_fungi,   fact = "Height",   conditions = "High",   effect_size = 3,   prop_taxa = 0.05,   seed = 123 )  # Library sizes are preserved all.equal(sample_sums(data_fungi), sample_sums(d_da))  # Test with ALDEx2 res <- MiscMetabar::aldex_pq(d_da,   bifactor = "Height",   modalities = c("Low", "High") ) gg_aldex_plot(res, type = "volcano")  # Check which taxa were made DA attr(d_da, "da_taxa") } # }

title: Rainplot of the nb taxa assigned (not NA) — rainplot_taxo_na
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. ranks (character integer vector, default NULL) ranks include rainplot. left NULL, ranks used. rank can defined either integer index full names (exactly matching colnames tax_table slot). min_nb_seq (integer, default 0) Minimum number sequences filter taxa low abundance. merge_sample_by (character, default NULL) vector determine samples merge using MiscMetabar::merge_samples2() function. Need physeq@sam_data. sample_colored (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, points colored samples. sample_linked (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, points linked samples. ... Additional arguments passed ggrain::geom_rain().

code: rainplot_taxo_na(subset_taxa_pq(Glom_otu, taxa_sums(Glom_otu) > 5000),   ranks = c("Family", "Family__eukaryome_Glomero") ) #> Cleaning suppress 0 taxa (  ) and 1 sample(s) ( samp_Blanc-PCR-racines ). #> Number of non-matching ASV 0 #> Number of matching ASV 1147 #> Number of filtered-out ASV 955 #> Number of kept ASV 192 #> Number of kept samples 443 #> Registered S3 methods overwritten by 'ggpp': #>   method                  from    #>   heightDetails.titleGrob ggplot2 #>   widthDetails.titleGrob  ggplot2  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ rainplot_taxo_na(Glom_otu)  Glom_otu@sam_data$tmt_type <- paste0(Glom_otu@sam_data$Tmt, "_", Glom_otu@sam_data$Type) rainplot_taxo_na(   Glom_otu,   merge_sample_by = "tmt_type",   sample_colored = TRUE,   sample_linked = TRUE ) rainplot_taxo_na(Glom_otu, ranks = c(4, 12), rain.side = "f1x1") rainplot_taxo_na(   Glom_otu,   ranks = c(6, 14),   rain.side = "f1x1",   sample_linked = TRUE ) +   theme(legend.position = "none") } # }

what: Resolve taxonomic conflict in the tax_table of a phyloseq object — resolve_taxo_conflict
title: Resolve taxonomic conflict in the tax_table of a phyloseq object — resolve_taxo_conflict
text: Resolve taxonomic conflict tax_table phyloseq object

title: Resolve taxonomic conflict in the tax_table of a phyloseq object — resolve_taxo_conflict
code: resolve_taxo_conflict(   physeq,   pattern_tax_ranks = NULL,   method = c("consensus", "rel_majority", "abs_majority", "preference", "unanimity"),   strict = FALSE,   second_method = c("consensus", "rel_majority", "abs_majority", "preference",     "unanimity"),   nb_agree_threshold = 1,   keep_tax_ranks = TRUE,   new_names = NULL,   preference_pattern = NULL,   collapse_string = "/",   replace_collapsed_rank_by_NA = FALSE )

title: Resolve taxonomic conflict in the tax_table of a phyloseq object — resolve_taxo_conflict
text: physeq (required) phyloseq object. pattern_tax_ranks (character vector, default NULL) vector patterns aggregate taxonomic ranks. example "^Genus" stands taxonomic ranks (columns tax_table slot) starting Genus. method (character, default "consensus") One "consensus", "rel_majority", "abs_majority", "preference" "unanimity". See details documentation function MiscMetabar::resolve_vector_ranks(). strict (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, NA considered informative resolving conflict (.e. NA taken account vote). See details information. second_method (character, default "consensus") One "consensus", "rel_majority", "abs_majority", "unanimity". used method = "preference". See details. nb_agree_threshold (integer, default 1) minimum number times value must arise selected using vote. 2, taxonomic values present least 2 times vector kept. keep_tax_ranks (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, keep old taxonomic ranks result. new_names (character vector, default NULL) vector new names taxonomic columns. preference_pattern (character, default NULL) pattern match column used preferred one method = "preference". collapse_string (character, default "/") character collapse taxonomic names multiple assignment done. replace_collapsed_rank_by_NA (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, multiple assignments (taxonomic rank including 'collapse_string' parameter) replaced NA.

what: Select taxonomic ranks in a phyloseq object — select_ranks_pq
title: Select taxonomic ranks in a phyloseq object — select_ranks_pq
text: Useful tidyverse-like pipeline.

title: Select taxonomic ranks in a phyloseq object — select_ranks_pq
code: select_ranks_pq(physeq, ...)

title: Select taxonomic ranks in a phyloseq object — select_ranks_pq
text: physeq (required) phyloseq object. ... One unquoted expressions separated commas. Variable names can used positions data frame, expressions like x:y can used select range variables. See ?dplyr::select.

title: Select taxonomic ranks in a phyloseq object — select_ranks_pq
text: phyloseq object

title: Select taxonomic ranks in a phyloseq object — select_ranks_pq
text: Adrien Taudière

title: Select taxonomic ranks in a phyloseq object — select_ranks_pq
code: select_ranks_pq(data_fungi, Order, Family)@tax_table |>   dim() #> [1] 1420    2 select_ranks_pq(data_fungi, !Order)@tax_table |>   colnames() #>  [1] "Domain"             "Phylum"             "Class"              #>  [4] "Family"             "Genus"              "Species"            #>  [7] "Trophic.Mode"       "Guild"              "Trait"              #> [10] "Confidence.Ranking" "Genus_species"      #

title: Display shared sample_data modalities — shared_mod_lpq

text: x (required) list_phyloseq object. max_modalities (integer, default 10) Maximum number modalities display per variable.

text: tibble NULL shared modalities exist tibble NULL shared modalities exist

what: Venn diagram of shared taxa across sample groups — simple_venn_pq
title: Venn diagram of shared taxa across sample groups — simple_venn_pq
text: Draws Venn diagram showing shared unique taxa (higher-rank groups) across 2 4 levels sample variable, using ggplot2 (external Venn diagram package needed). taxonomic_rank character vector length > 1 (default), ranks displayed single combined figure using patchwork package (must installed). Set combine = FALSE get named list individual plots instead. 2 3 groups, circles used. 4 groups, ellipses used ensure intersection regions representable.

title: Venn diagram of shared taxa across sample groups — simple_venn_pq
code: simple_venn_pq(   physeq,   fact = NULL,   min_nb_seq = 0,   taxonomic_rank = c("Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species"),   na_remove = TRUE,   count_type = c("rank", "taxa", "sequences", "rank_taxa"),   add_nb_samples = TRUE,   fill_alpha = 0.3,   border_size = 0.8,   text_size = 4,   scale_text = FALSE,   hide_zero = TRUE,   label_size = 4.5,   colors = NULL,   show_na_count = FALSE,   count_taxa = TRUE,   match_by = c("refseq", "names"),   combine = TRUE,   verbose = TRUE,   .lpq_n_samples = NULL )

title: Venn diagram of shared taxa across sample groups — simple_venn_pq
text: physeq (phyloseq list_phyloseq, required) phyloseq object, list_phyloseq object. list_phyloseq provided, first merged single phyloseq using merge_lpq() (original phyloseq becomes one sample) fact parameter automatically set "source_name". fact (character, required physeq phyloseq) Name variable sample_data(physeq) defining groups (2-4 levels). Ignored physeq list_phyloseq. min_nb_seq (integer, default 0) Minimum total read count taxon considered present group. taxon must strictly min_nb_seq reads group included. taxonomic_rank (character NULL) Taxonomic rank(s) aggregate (via phyloseq::tax_glom()) computing Venn diagram. Defaults standard ranks (Kingdom Species). Use NULL skip aggregation work ASV/OTU level. na_remove (logical, default TRUE) Remove samples NA fact , aggregating, taxa NA taxonomic_rank. count_type (character, default "rank") count Venn region. One : "rank": number unique taxonomic levels (e.g. number shared Classes). default. "taxa": number ASVs/OTUs assigned shared taxonomic levels. "sequences": total number reads ASVs/OTUs assigned shared taxonomic levels. "rank_taxa": shows rank taxa counts "nb_rank (nb_taxa)". Ignored taxonomic_rank NULL (ASV-level), "rank" "taxa" equivalent. add_nb_samples (logical, default TRUE) Append sample count group labels. fill_alpha (numeric, default 0.3) Fill transparency shapes. border_size (numeric, default 0.8) Border line width. text_size (numeric, default 4) Base size count labels inside regions. scale_text (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, scale size count labels proportionally count value. text_size parameter acts base (minimum) size. hide_zero (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, hide count labels zero ("0 (0)" count_type = "rank_taxa"). label_size (numeric, default 4.5) Size group name labels. colors (character NULL) Vector colors, one per group. Defaults 4-color qualitative palette. show_na_count (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, display number taxa NA chosen taxonomic_rank bottom-left corner plot. count_type = "taxa", sum Venn region counts plus NA count equals ntaxa(physeq). Ignored taxonomic_rank NULL. count_taxa (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, append "Taxa" panel Venn diagram showing shared unique individual taxa (ASVs/OTUs) alongside aggregated taxonomic ranks. temporary Taxa column added tax_table taxon's name value. Ignored taxonomic_rank NULL. match_by (character, default "refseq") Passed merge_lpq() physeq list_phyloseq. One "refseq" "names". combine (logical, default TRUE) taxonomic_rank length > 1, combine plots single patchwork figure. Set FALSE return named list individual ggplot objects. Requires patchwork package. verbose (logical, default TRUE) Print message taxa meet criteria.

title: Venn diagram of shared taxa across sample groups — simple_venn_pq
text: ggplot2 object (single rank), patchwork object (multiple ranks combine = TRUE), named list ggplot2 objects (multiple ranks combine = FALSE).

title: Venn diagram of shared taxa across sample groups — simple_venn_pq
text: Adrien Taudiere

title: Venn diagram of shared taxa across sample groups — simple_venn_pq
code: # Default: all ranks combined in one figure simple_venn_pq(data_fungi_mini, "Height")   # At genus level only simple_venn_pq(data_fungi_mini, "Height", taxonomic_rank = "Genus")   # Multiple ranks as a list plots <- simple_venn_pq(   data_fungi_mini, "Height",   taxonomic_rank = c("Family", "Genus"),   combine = FALSE ) plots[["Family"]]   # Count ASVs instead of rank levels simple_venn_pq(data_fungi_mini, "Height",   taxonomic_rank = "Genus", count_type = "taxa" )   # Scale text by count value simple_venn_pq(data_fungi_mini, "Height",   taxonomic_rank = "Genus", scale_text = TRUE )   # From a list_phyloseq object lpq <- list_phyloseq(list(   fungi = data_fungi_mini,   fungi2 = data_fungi )) #> ℹ Building summary table for 2 phyloseq objects... #> ℹ Computing comparison characteristics... #> ℹ Checking sample and taxa overlap... #> ℹ Detected comparison type: NESTED_ROBUSTNESS #> ℹ 137 common samples, 45 common taxa #> ✔ list_phyloseq created (NESTED_ROBUSTNESS) simple_venn_pq(lpq, taxonomic_rank = "Genus", count_taxa) #> Merging 2 phyloseq objects by refseq: 45 + 1420 taxa -> 1420 unique sequences.

title: Convert taxonomy dataframe to phylogenetic tree — taxo2tree

title: Convert taxonomy dataframe to phylogenetic tree — taxo2tree
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object ranks Character vector specifying column names use taxonomic ranks, ordered highest lowest. default: c("Domain", "Phylum", "Class", "Order", "Family", "Genus", "Species"). internal_node_singletons Logical, TRUE, create internal nodes singleton. FALSE, internal nodes one descendant discarded. use_taxa_names Logical, TRUE (default), use taxa names (rownames, e.g., ASV_1, ASV_2) terminal leaves. FALSE, collapse identical taxonomy paths use lowest rank value tip labels. useful creating cleaner trees show taxonomic structure without individual ASV/OTU names.

what: Compute congruence metrics between two taxonomic assignments — tc_congruence_metrics
title: Compute congruence metrics between two taxonomic assignments — tc_congruence_metrics
text: Computes metrics quantifying congruence two taxonomic assignments set taxa (ASVs/OTUs). useful comparing different taxonomic databases, assignment methods, reference versions.

title: Compute congruence metrics between two taxonomic assignments — tc_congruence_metrics
code: tc_congruence_metrics(physeq_1, physeq_2 = NULL, ranks_1, ranks_2 = NULL)

title: Compute congruence metrics between two taxonomic assignments — tc_congruence_metrics
text: physeq_1 (required) phyloseq-class object first taxonomic assignment. physeq_2 (phyloseq, default NULL) phyloseq-class object second taxonomic assignment. NULL, uses physeq_1 (useful comparing different rank columns object). ranks_1 (character vector, required) Taxonomic rank names use first assignment. Must match column names physeq_1's tax_table. ranks_2 (character vector, default NULL) Taxonomic rank names use second assignment. Must match column names physeq_2's tax_table. NULL, uses ranks ranks_1 vector.

title: Compute congruence metrics between two taxonomic assignments — tc_congruence_metrics
text: list following components: summary data frame counts percentages category, including leaf_match_congruence counts taxa deepest classification matches regardless path only_in_1 Character vector taxa names present physeq_1 only_in_2 Character vector taxa names present physeq_2 classified_only_1 Character vector common taxa classified physeq_1 (NA physeq_2 specified ranks) classified_only_2 Character vector common taxa classified physeq_2 (NA physeq_1 specified ranks) unclassified_both Character vector common taxa NA physeq objects total_congruent Character vector taxa identical taxonomic paths (values ranks, NA positions) partial_1_deeper Character vector taxa physeq_1 classifies deeper rank agrees shared ranks partial_2_deeper Character vector taxa physeq_2 classifies deeper rank agrees shared ranks incongruent_leaves Character vector taxa disagreement deepest (leaf) level assignments incongruent_nodes Character vector taxa disagreement higher (internal node) levels, even leaves match details data frame per-taxon details including: taxon name, depth_1, depth_2, leaf_1, leaf_2, leaf_match (TRUE leaves equal), category

what: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
title: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
text: Creates visualization two taxonomy trees facing , matching taxa aligned horizontally. Optionally draws lines connecting matching leaf taxa. Useful comparing taxonomic assignments different databases classification methods.

title: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
code: tc_linked_trees(   physeq_1,   physeq_2 = NULL,   ranks_1,   ranks_2,   tree_distance = 1,   show_tip_links = TRUE,   link_by_taxa = FALSE,   link_alpha = 0.5,   link_color = "grey50",   show_labels = TRUE,   label_size = 2,   internal_node_singletons = FALSE,   use_taxa_names = FALSE )

title: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
text: physeq_1 (required) phyloseq-class object left tree. physeq_2 (phyloseq, default NULL) phyloseq-class object right tree. NULL, uses physeq_1 (useful comparing different rank columns object). ranks_1 (character vector, required) Taxonomic rank names use first tree. Must match column names physeq_1's tax_table. ranks_2 (character vector, required) Taxonomic rank names use second tree. Must match column names physeq_2's tax_table. tree_distance (numeric, default 1) Distance two trees. show_tip_links (logical, default TRUE) Whether draw lines matching leaf taxa (tips). Internal nodes never linked. link_by_taxa (logical, default FALSE) TRUE, links drawn based taxa (ASV/OTU) correspondence: taxon physeq_1, link drawn corresponding tip tree_2 based taxonomy. Line width proportional number taxa sharing link. Requires physeq_1 physeq_2 taxa names (rownames). link_alpha (numeric, default 0.5) Transparency correspondence lines. link_color (character, default "grey50") Color correspondence lines. Use NULL color label. show_labels (logical, default TRUE) Whether show node/tip labels. label_size (numeric, default 2) Base size labels. Labels scaled depth: shallower nodes (closer root) get larger text (1.5x), deeper nodes get smaller text (0.6x). labels positioned branch avoid overlap. internal_node_singletons (logical, default FALSE) Whether create internal nodes singletons. Passed taxo2tree. use_taxa_names (logical, default FALSE) Whether use taxa names (e.g., ASV_1, ASV_2) terminal leaves. FALSE (default), collapses identical taxonomy paths uses lowest rank value tip labels. Passed taxo2tree.

title: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
text: ggplot2 object can customized.

title: Plot two taxonomy trees with linked correspondences — tc_linked_trees
text: Adrien Taudière

code: # Compare two taxonomic assignments from the same phyloseq object # using different rank columns (physeq_2 defaults to physeq_1) tc_linked_trees(   Glom_otu,   ranks_1 = c("Kingdom", "Class", "Order", "Family"),   ranks_2 = c(     "Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero", "Class__eukaryome_Glomero",     "Order__eukaryome_Glomero", "Family__eukaryome_Glomero"   ) ) #> Found more than one class "phylo" in cache; using the first, from namespace 'phyloseq' #> Also defined by ‘tidytree’ #> Found more than one class "phylo" in cache; using the first, from namespace 'phyloseq' #> Also defined by ‘tidytree’   if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Link by taxa with line width proportional to ASV count tc_linked_trees(   Glom_otu,   ranks_1 = c("Kingdom", "Class", "Order", "Family"),   ranks_2 = c(     "Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero", "Class__eukaryome_Glomero",     "Order__eukaryome_Glomero", "Family__eukaryome_Glomero"   ),   link_by_taxa = TRUE )  # Without tip links tc_linked_trees(   Glom_otu,   ranks_1 = c("Kingdom", "Class", "Order", "Family"),   ranks_2 = c(     "Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero", "Class__eukaryome_Glomero",     "Order__eukaryome_Glomero", "Family__eukaryome_Glomero"   ),   show_tip_links = FALSE )  # Color links by label tc_linked_trees(   Glom_otu,   ranks_1 = c("Kingdom", "Class", "Order", "Family"),   ranks_2 = c(     "Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero", "Class__eukaryome_Glomero",     "Order__eukaryome_Glomero", "Family__eukaryome_Glomero"   ),   link_color = NULL ) +   theme(legend.position = "none")  # Using one phyloseq object for both trees # and different rank levels with link_by_taxa tc_linked_trees(   Glom_otu,   ranks_1 = c("Kingdom", "Class", "Order", "Family"),   ranks_2 = c(     "Kingdom__eukaryome_Glomero", "Class__eukaryome_Glomero",     "Order__eukaryome_Glomero", "Family__eukaryome_Glomero"   ), link_color = NULL,   link_by_taxa = TRUE, ) +   theme(legend.position = "none") } # }

title: Compute accuracy metrics of taxonomic assignation using a mock (known) community for one rank — tc_metrics_mock_vec
text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. taxonomic_rank (required) Name (number) taxonomic rank count. true_values (required) vector true taxonomic assignation fake_taxa (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, fake_pattern vector used identify fake taxa, .e. taxa reference database (see add_external_seq_pq()) taxa fake sequences (see add_shuffle_seq_pq()). fake_pattern (character vector, default c("^fake_", "^external_")) vector patterns used identify fake taxa using regex search name. verbose (logical, default TRUE) TRUE, print informative messages.

title: Compute accuracy metrics of taxonomic assignation using a mock (known) community for one rank — tc_metrics_mock_vec
text: list metrics (see confusion matrix article wikipedia): TP (number true positive) FP (number false positive) FN (number false negative) FDR (false discovery rate) = FP / (FP + TP) TPR (true positive rate, also named recall sensitivity) PPV (positive predictive value, also named precision) = TP / (TP + FP) F1_score (F1 score) = 2 * TP / (2 * TP + FP + FN) fake taxa present fake_taxa true, metrics computed: TN (number true negative) ACC (Accuracy) = (TP + TN) / (TP + TN + FP + FN) MCC (Matthews correlation coefficient) = (TP * TN - FP * FN) / sqrt((TP + FP) * (TP + FN) * (FP + TN) * (TN + FN))

title: Compute accuracy metrics of taxonomic assignation using a mock (known) community for one rank — tc_metrics_mock_vec
text: Adrien Taudière

text: physeq (required) phyloseq-class object obtained using phyloseq package. rank_1 (character integer) Define first taxonomic rank number name column tax_table slot. rank_2 (character integer) Define second taxonomic rank number name column tax_table slot. color_1 (character, default "#dc863b") Color rank_1 values. color_2 (character, default "#2e7891") Color rank_2 values. stat_across_sample (character, default "sum") Either "mean" "sum". Set abundance computed across samples. merge_sample_by (character, default NULL) vector determine samples merge using MiscMetabar::merge_samples2() function. Need physeq@sam_data.

code: data("enterotype", package = "phyloseq") lpq <- list_phyloseq(list(   fung = data_fungi,   fung_mini = data_fungi_mini,   fung_rarefy = rarefy_even_depth(data_fungi),   enterotype = enterotype )) #> You set `rngseed` to FALSE. Make sure you've set & recorded #>  the random seed of your session for reproducibility. #> See `?set.seed` #> ... #> 1016OTUs were removed because they are no longer  #> present in any sample after random subsampling #> ... #> ℹ Building summary table for 4 phyloseq objects... #> ℹ Computing comparison characteristics... #> ℹ Checking sample and taxa overlap... #> ℹ Detected comparison type: NESTED_ROBUSTNESS #> ℹ 0 common samples, 0 common taxa #> ℹ Skipping refseq comparison (not all objects have refseq) #> ✔ list_phyloseq created (NESTED_ROBUSTNESS) upset_lpq(lpq, plot_type = "upset") #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the ComplexUpset package. #>   Please report the issue at #>   <https://github.com/krassowski/complex-upset/issues>.  lpq2 <- list_phyloseq(list(   fung = data_fungi,   fung_mini = data_fungi_mini )) #> ℹ Building summary table for 2 phyloseq objects... #> ℹ Computing comparison characteristics... #> ℹ Checking sample and taxa overlap... #> ℹ Detected comparison type: NESTED_ROBUSTNESS #> ℹ 137 common samples, 45 common taxa #> ✔ list_phyloseq created (NESTED_ROBUSTNESS) upset_lpq(lpq2, tax_rank = "Family")

title: comparpq 0.1.3 (Development version)
text: Add param compute_dist list_phyloseq()

title: comparpq 0.1.2
text: Add params significance, test p_alpha div_pq() report tuckey hsd paired-test using letters. gg_hill_lpq() new function visualize Hill diversity correlations across pairs phyloseq objects list_phyloseq. Produces faceted scatter plot (pairs × Hill orders) optional 1:1 line, regression line, per-panel correlation annotation, enabling visual assessment REPRODUCIBILITY, ROBUSTNESS, REPLICABILITY. gg_bubbles_pq() now uses merge_lpq() list_phyloseq passed, merging single phyloseq faceting source_name instead building separate patchwork panels. diff_contour now works facet_by variable (just list_phyloseq), highlighting taxa unique facet level distinct contour color diff_contour_colors. longer limited 2 3 objects. New match_by parameter controls taxa matched merging list_phyloseq objects. gg_bubbles_pq() new ggplot2-based circle-packed bubble plot taxa abundances. Unlike bubbles_pq(), require d3js/Observable supports faceting @sam_data variable display one bubble chart per level. Uses packcircles layout computation. compare_refseq() new function compare reference sequences (refseq slot) two phyloseq objects, identifying shared unique ASVs/OTUs name DNA sequence content, including detection -name-different-sequence -sequence-different-name mismatches. Computes mean nearest-neighbor k-mer distance unique sequences. Add function apply_to_lpq() apply function phyloseq object list_phyloseq estim_cor_lpq() new function compute bootstrap correlation/regression across list_phyloseq estim_cor_pq() new function compute bootstrap correlation regression CIs diversity vs numeric variables estim_diff_lpq() new function run estimation statistics (effect sizes + CIs) across list_phyloseq estim_diff_pq() new function estimation statistics (Gardner-Altman/Cumming plots) comparing diversity across groups via dabestr merge_lpq() new function merge list_phyloseq single phyloseq object original phyloseq becomes one sample. Taxa can matched reference sequences (match_by = "refseq", default) taxa names (match_by = "names"). simple_venn_pq() new function draw Venn diagrams shared taxa across 2-4 sample groups using pure ggplot2 (external Venn package needed), support multiple taxonomic ranks compact, clearly labeled circles/ellipses. simple_venn_pq() now accepts list_phyloseq object input, automatically merging via merge_lpq() drawing Venn diagram.
